title: Class Declaration
code:
class Student {
};
explanation: A class is a user-defined data type.

title: Object Creation
description: Create an object of a class
code: Student s1;
explanation: An object is an instance of a class.

title: Public Members
explanation: Public members can be accessed from outside the class.

title: Private Members
explanation: Private members can only be accessed within the class.

title: Protected Members
explanation: Protected members are accessible in derived classes.

title: Default Constructor
explanation: Called automatically when object is created.

title: Parameterized Constructor
explanation: Used to initialize object with values.

title: Copy Constructor
explanation: Creates a new object as a copy of another object.

title: Destructor
explanation: Automatically called when object is destroyed.

title: This Pointer
explanation: Refers to the current object.

title: Getter Function
explanation: Getter returns the value of a private member.

title: Setter Function
explanation: Setter updates the value of a private member.

title: Encapsulation
explanation: Encapsulation combines data and methods while restricting direct access.

title: Inheritance
explanation: Derived class inherits properties and methods of base class.

title: Single Inheritance
description: One base class and one derived class
code:
class A {};
class B : public A {};
explanation: A derived class inherits from one base class.

title: Multiple Inheritance
explanation: A class inherits from more than one base class.

title: Multilevel Inheritance
explanation: A derived class becomes the base for another class.

title: Hierarchical Inheritance
description: Multiple derived classes from one base class
explanation: Several classes inherit from the same base class.

title: Hybrid Inheritance
explanation: Combines multiple inheritance models.

title: Inheritance Constructor Call
explanation: Base constructor can be called using initializer list.

title: Method Overriding
description: Derived class redefines base class method
explanation: Derived class provides its own implementation.

title: Virtual Function
explanation: Allows method overriding to work through base pointers.

title: Pure Virtual Function
explanation: Forces derived classes to implement the function.

title: Abstract Class
explanation: Objects of abstract classes cannot be created.

title: Runtime Polymorphism
code:
Animal* a = new Dog();
a->sound();
explanation: Function call resolved at runtime.

title: Compile Time Polymorphism
code:
int add(int a, int b);
double add(double a, double b);
explanation: Function overloading is compile-time polymorphism.

title: Function Overloading
code:
void print(int x){}
void print(string s){}
explanation: Compiler chooses function based on arguments.

title: Operator Overloading
explanation: Provides custom behavior for operators.

title: Virtual Destructor
explanation: Ensures proper cleanup through base pointers.

title: Dynamic Binding
code:
Base* ptr = new Derived();
ptr->show();
explanation: Actual function is determined during execution.

title: Friend Function
description: Friend function can access private members
explanation: Friend functions can access private and protected members.

title: Friend Class
explanation: All members of the friend class can access private members.

title: Static Variable
explanation: Static variables belong to the class, not objects.

title: Static Function
explanation: Can be called without creating an object.

title: Composition
explanation: Strong ownership relationship.

title: Aggregation
explanation: Weak ownership relationship.

title: Association
code:
class Teacher {};
class Student {};
explanation: Represents a general relationship between objects.

title: Nested Class
explanation: Useful for logically grouping classes.

title: Object Array
code: Student arr[10];
explanation: Stores multiple objects in an array.

title: Object Pointer
code:
Student s;
Student* ptr = &s;
explanation: Stores address of an object.

title: Shallow Copy
explanation: Copies memory address, not actual data.

title: Deep Copy
description: Create separate memory for copied object
explanation: Creates independent copies of dynamic memory.

title: Move Constructor
explanation: Avoids expensive deep copies.

title: Move Assignment Operator
explanation: Transfers ownership during assignment.

title: Unique Pointer
code: unique_ptr<int> ptr = make_unique<int>(10);
explanation: Only one owner can exist at a time.

title: Shared Pointer
code: shared_ptr<int> ptr = make_shared<int>(10);
explanation: Multiple pointers can own the same object.

title: Weak Pointer
code: weak_ptr<int> wp = sp;
explanation: Prevents circular references with shared_ptr.

title: RAII Pattern
explanation: Resources are tied to object lifetime.

title: Rule Of Three
explanation: If one is needed, usually all three are needed.

title: Rule Of Five
explanation: Extends Rule of Three with move operations.
